Title: Discovering the LLM's curious and remarkable world knowledge of open data on the web.

Title: My son, the OpenAI server - byandrev
Description: Sam Altman has said that he finds the comparison made regarding the energy consumption of artificial intelligence unfair, and that no one questions how much energy is used to "train" a human.

Title: Evals or You're Blind — Pydantic AI × Pydantic Evals × crabbox

Title: How to Just Do a Thing

Title: I Built a Local News Website
Description: The Boise started as a personal experiment in local media, AI, maps, and UX. It became a way to rethink what local news can feel like when it is built around how people actually live.

Title: Structure of Every LLM Chat
Description: Role tagging is not cosmetic. It shapes how the model responds, how context is managed across multiple turns, and how application developers constrain and direct model behaviour at a structural level. Understanding this format is the difference between using an LLM and building reliably on top of one.

Title: For EU Open Source, the Budget Is the Policy
Description: The EU's new Open Source Strategy is a real step forward, but it still has no procurement teeth. Here is what changed since 2020, what hasn't, and why the budget Brussels already controls is the policy that matters most.

Title: OpenAI has a branding problem
Description: The company that led the consumer AI race might be losing the naming one.

Title: Quill Grid
Description: Quill Grid: The Starlink for AI becomes a network. An optional, opt-in distributed AI grid where idle Quill One devices share spare capacity, earn credits, and donate AI hours.

Title: You Don't Need an Outbox - EventSourcingDB
Description: The outbox pattern has quietly become canonical in microservice tutorials. It has a name, library support, conference talks, and a steady stream of blog posts that walk through implementing it. It's blessed. And yet, every time it shows up in a system, it's a sign that something else has gone wrong upstream.

Title: Building with Agents — Readables
Description: Agents get you to 90% fast. The last 10% is where the engineering lives — and it still belongs to you. Notes on the last mile, decisions, and scope creep.

Title: The Approach to Equilibrium
Description: Coffee cools, eggs scramble, the past never comes back. Yet the laws underneath run just as well in reverse. Here is the simplest machine I know that holds both truths at once, and shows where the missing order really goes.

Title: The Agentic Test Pyramid — Matthew Boston
Description: The classic test pyramid assumes every test is deterministic. The moment a model is part of your system, that assumption breaks. You need a second axis.

Title: Opus 4.8 vs Opus 4.7 vs GPT-5.5 vs Composer 2.5 - 50 Real PRs in Go and Rust
Description: I graded four frontier coding models on 50 real merged PRs in Go and Rust - not just whether tests pass, but craft, equivalence, and cost. Opus 4.8 led on craft in both.

Title: An Open Source Motorized XYZ Micro-Manipulator - Affordable sub µm Motion Control
Description:
A low-cost and easy to build 3-Axis motion plattform is presented. It can achieve step sizes down to 50nm while supporting arbitrary acceleration limited motion paths in a working volume of 23x23x23mm³. The simple G-Coder interface allows easy control from printer-hosts or custom software. The device can be used in applications, like automated microscopy, optical alignment or maskless lithography.

✅ Project on Github: https://github.com/0x23/MicroManipulatorStepper

🠲 I am looking for a better project name? Any ideas are welcome.
